title: BLIF backend aborts on case with single branch
body:
This code

```
module mod();

reg state, next_state;

always @* begin
  next_state = 0;
  case (state)
    0: next_state = state;
  endcase
end

endmodule
```

causes Iverilog to die with a message

```
$ iverilog -Wall -tblif mod.v
mod.v:3: assert: /home/Yura/src/iverilog/synth2.cc:668: failed assertion sel_need >= 2
```

The fix may be as simple as

```
diff --git a/synth2.cc b/synth2.cc
index b42fc44..709043f 100644
--- a/synth2.cc
+++ b/synth2.cc
@@ -854,6 +854,10 @@ bool NetCase::synth_async(Design*des, NetScope*scope,
       if (default_statement && (sel_width > sel_need))
            sel_need += 1;

+      // Case statement with a single branch
+      if (sel_need < 2)
+           sel_need = 2;
+
        // The mux size is always an exact power of 2.
       if (sel_need >= 8*sizeof(unsigned)) {
           cerr << get_fileline() << ": sorry: mux select width of "
```

This will be suboptimal when sel_need == 0 (i.e. single comparison to 0) because in that case you can keep just 1 bit. But this case is not practical so I think it's not a big deal.
code_blocks:
$ iverilog -Wall -tblif mod.v
mod.v:3: assert: /home/Yura/src/iverilog/synth2.cc:668: failed assertion sel_need >= 2
